title: Adapting connections of a layered network
abstract: A computer-implemented method for adapting connections between nodes of a layered network. The layered network comprises a plurality of nodes arranged in an ordered set of layers. The ordered set of layers comprises, in order, a core layer comprising a set of core nodes, a second layer comprising a set of second nodes, and one or more outer layers each comprising a respective set of outer nodes. Each core node is connected to at least one other core node. The method is performed by an adapting node, the adapting node being a node of the layered network that is connected to one or more second nodes and multiple core nodes. The method comprises, based on one or more network properties of the layered network, disabling a respective connection between the adapting node and at least one but not all of the multiple core nodes.
claims: 1. A computer-implemented method for adapting connections between nodes of a layered network, wherein the layered network comprises a plurality of nodes arranged in an ordered set of layers, the ordered set of layers comprising, in order, a core layer comprising a set of core nodes, a second layer comprising a set of second nodes, and one or more outer layers each comprising a respective set of outer nodes, wherein each core node is connected to at least one other core node, wherein the method is performed by an adapting node, the adapting node being a node of the layered network that is connected to one or more second nodes and multiple core nodes, and wherein the method comprises: based on one or more network properties of the layered network, disabling a respective connection between the adapting node and at least one but not all of the multiple core nodes, wherein the network properties comprise a number of connections between a given core node and the other nodes of the layered network being above a predetermined threshold.
